spec_match.rationale: Chromium-catches-up feature — the spec already documents the behaviour, so the value of the demo is the interop story. The concept exercises one corner but the before/after comparison isn't always crisp.

interactive.rationale: No real form controls driving the script — page is closer to a styled code card than an interactive demo.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates the feature-index blurb's key promise (overlap on arabic, canonical, character, comprehensions).

edge_cases.rationale: Demo runs the happy path; no visible feature detection, error handling, or no-support fallback in the script.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/6317308531965952 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from the shared CSS variables and the layout follows the stage + controls editorial pattern.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: operators with no mirror codepoint (glyph-level) vs paired b
openQuestions.detail: The mirroring-of-rtl-mathml-operators feature's headline framing — Character-level (codepoint swap) and glyph-level mirroring for MathML operators in RTL mode — calls out "operators with no mirror codepoint (glyph-level) vs paired brackets (character-level)". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: real-world Arabic math formulae
openQuestions.detail: The mirroring-of-rtl-mathml-operators feature's headline framing — Character-level (codepoint swap) and glyph-level mirroring for MathML operators in RTL mode — calls out "real-world Arabic math formulae". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Add real controls so the demo isn't a styled code card
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages are supposed to be hands-on. With only 0 button(s), 0 input(s), 0 select(s), 0 range slider(s), the surface is thin. Add at least one slider, picker, or interactive readout so visitors can actually probe the API from the page.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

summary: textbook-gallery under mirroring-of-rtl-mathml-operators: 3 pass / 2 partial / 1 fail. Interactive surface is thin, design system clean, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
